repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: National Institute of Child Health and Human Development (U.S.)

did.abstract: Files relating to the career of Henry N. Ricciuti, Professor of Human Development and Family Studies in the New York State College of Human Ecology.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor of Human Development and Family Studies, New York State College of Human Ecology.

Henry Ricciuti earned his B.S., M.A. and Ph.D. (1949), all in psychology from Fordham University, and served in the U.S. Army from 1943 to 1946. Prior to joining the Cornell faculty in 1958, he served as an assistant professor at the University of Connecticut, research associate at the Educational Testing Service, and assistant professor at the University of Colorado Medical School. At Cornell, he was a professor in the Dept. of Human Development and Family Studies (1958-1988), specialized in early childhood development, especially the factors associated with cognitive and emotional development of infants and young children. He was an active emeritus professor from 1988-2011.

emph.ref: Series I. College of Home Economics Organization Committee
Series II. Faculty Committee on Student Affairs (FCSA)
Series III. Infant Day Care and Resource Center
Series IV. Cornell-Tompkins Day Care Project
Series XIV. National Institute of Child Health and Human Development (NICHD)

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Henry N. Ricciuti papers, #23-13-2551. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Files relating to the reorganization of the College of Human Ecology, particularly the Reorganization Committee, chaired by Dr. Ricciuti; files relating to his membership on the Faculty Committee on Student Affairs, 1966-1969, with much material on events leading up to the Straight takeover in April 1969; and materials relating to Cornell's involvement with day care, starting with the Cornell Day Care Committee, 1971-1972; the Cornell-Tompkins County Day Care Project, 1972-1974; the Infant Care and Resource Center, operated in Sage Infirmary, 1975-1980, under a grant from the Appalachian Regional Commission; and the unsuccessful efforts to continue to offer work-site infant day care on campus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Includes material relating to the National Institute of Child Health and Human Developmpent (NICHD).

Employer-supported day care.

International Workshop on Nutrition and Child Development at the University of Baroda, India

Exploring position change - from Educational Testing Service, Princeton
Exploring job openings from the University of Colorado
University of Colorado, Child Research Council

Series XIV. National Institute of Child Health and Human Development (NICHD)
NICHD Steering Committee and publications and papers

Memos, notes and correspondence from service as chairman of the Steering Committee guiding the study (1989-05-1991-05)and subsequently as a member of the Advisory Board appointed by and reporting to the Director of the National Institute of Child Health and Human Development (1991-08-2005-05)
